summary: Excellent matrix recording of the legendary Boston ’73 show, highlighted by a soaring Eyes of the World and flawless sound
review: The Dusborne matrix is consistently praised as one of the best ever – a true blend of clean soundboard and audience that captures the band’s energy with crystal‑clear clarity.  The performance itself is lauded as a 22‑song monster, featuring an unforgettable 19‑minute Eyes of the World, tight transitions, and superb interplay across both new material and classic staples.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 20 detailed reviews uniformly award 4‑5 stars, extolling both the musical brilliance (especially the Eyes jam) and the superb matrix sound; no substantive criticisms were found.

recording_quality.technical_notes: True matrix of soundboard overlaid with audience, clean mix, no noticeable drop‑outs or muddiness; reviewers call it the best matrix they’ve heard.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A 22‑song marathon with tight transitions, a great mix of newer material and classic standards; reviewers note the show’s pacing as “exactly perfect” and “monster sweet”.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeated praise for his guitar tone and overall playing – described as “perfect” and “energy‑filled”.
band_member_comments.Bob: Positive remarks on his vocal delivery and a humorous “Donnaskreech” imitation during Sugar Magnolia.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith is highlighted on piano, especially on the Weather Report Suite; his playing adds depth to the mix.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s jazzy style is praised; Mickey Hart is noted as absent but not a detriment.
